title: Navitas Semiconductor Stock's AI Data Center Pivot: Up 88% YTD—Is NVTS Stock Still a Buy?
text: Navitas Semiconductor has experienced an 88% year-to-date rally following its strategic pivot from consumer charging markets to high-power applications including AI data centers and grid infrastructure. Despite this impressive gain, the stock declined after Q1 earnings were reported on May 5, 2026. The company is deliberately transitioning away from the low-margin, extremely commoditized China mobile-charging business to focus on higher-value markets targeting AI data centers, energy infrastructure, high-performance computing, and industrial electrification—collectively referred to as Navitas 2.0. Navitas differentiates itself through monolithic integration of GaN power devices, drivers, control, and protection on a single chip. The company also offers both GaN and SiC technology portfolios, enabling broader market coverage. A notable partnership with NVIDIA positions Navitas as a power semiconductor partner for next-generation AI factory platforms, with collaborative work on 800V HVDC architectures. Financial challenges from the pivot include near-term financial strain through restructuring charges and revenue contraction as legacy business winds down. Design wins don't automatically convert to volume orders, with management expecting late 2025 as the revenue trough and meaningful inflection delayed until 2027. At approximately $2 billion market cap against a projected $3.5 billion serviceable market by 2030, the analysis suggests the reward-to-risk ratio does not present a tantalizing opportunity for most investors.
